title: Traffic-aware relative field-state features
summary: Public F1 strategy sources emphasize that pit timing depends on clear air, nearby rivals, pit-window timing, and whether a car is within undercut/overcut range, not just absolute tyre age.
rationale: Public F1 strategy sources emphasize that pit timing depends on clear air, nearby rivals, pit-window timing, and whether a car is within undercut/overcut range, not just absolute tyre age. The current best code is mostly absolute-state engineering.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber` or narrow `RaceProgress` bucket, add percentile ranks/z-scores for `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, `TyreLife`, and `Position_Change`. Add local-congestion proxies such as counts of cars in nearby `Position`, same `Compound`, or same `Stint`, plus relative-to-race-median deltas. Keep CV grouped by race to avoid same-race leakage.
expected_effect: Better separation of true undercut/overcut opportunities from generic old-tyre laps; likely improves generalization more than adding new hard thresholds.
risk: Without actual interval gaps, traffic proxies may be noisy. Over-granular race-lap grouping can add variance on sparse laps.
